(2) Fun Frontend Effects With GSAP3 - Setup
First post in the series — let's set up the environment so we don't trip over a bunch of gotchas later.
All examples in this series live on CodePen. If you're not familiar with CodePen, I'd recommend reading my earlier post
first — otherwise you might hit CodePen-specific issues.
I've prepared a starter CodePen environment with some basic setup so you can fork it and practice without redoing the config every time.
You might be thinking: "Why Tailwind CSS?"
Just for convenience — Tailwind makes it easier to lay things out in CodePen demos. If you don't want it, you can drop the CDN.
Paste this into CodePen to test if GSAP is loading:
gsap.to('#box', { x: 100, duration: 1, repeat: -1, yoyo: true })
Don't worry about what the code does yet — that comes later. Just confirm the effect runs.
See the Pen GSAP setup by Ray (@hsiangfeng) on CodePen.

You should see a black square sliding left and right. If that's working, both GSAP and Tailwind are loading correctly. If not, let's debug first before moving on, otherwise none of the upcoming examples will work.
If both are loading and you still don't see the effect, it might be a network issue — try a different network and retry.
If you're not familiar with Tailwind CSS, maybe I'll write a Tailwind series someday too (lol).
That's it for setup. See you in the next post.
